key_insight: Atomic Cluster Expansion (ACE) potentials can greatly improve atomistic predictions of screw dislocation glide in bcc refractory metals, but accuracy depends strongly on database coverage and element-specific energetics.
engineering_relevance: These improved potentials enable more reliable multiscale modeling of plastic flow, Peierls barriers, and kink-pair energetics in refractory metals used for high-temperature structural applications.
method: Extended an existing DFT database for group 5 and 6 refractory metals (V, Nb, Ta, Mo, W).
Trained a family of ACE interatomic potentials targeting screw dislocation core and glide behavior.
Evaluated against DFT benchmarks for Peierls barriers, glide planes, and kink-pair activation enthalpies.
evidence_signal: The ACE models achieve near-DFT screw-dislocation predictions for Mo and W, with substantial improvement for V, Nb, and Ta; for Nb, Mo, and W they also reproduce kink-pair activation enthalpies consistent with Kocks' law and a line-tension model.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not sufficient to guarantee correct dislocation glide, so validation must include glide-plane behavior and broader transferability tests.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
